title: Vitis pyrrhodasys

Vitis pyrrhodasys is often associated with the section Euvitis, which includes species with woody vines and deciduous leaves. The classification of Vitis pyrrhodasys is a subject of ongoing botanical research and may be subject to revision.

Vitis vinifera, the common grapevine, is a climbing plant with edible fruits and leaves. The health benefits of Vitis vinifera include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease, cancer, and neurodegenerative disorders. The bioactive constituents of Vitis vinifera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and resveratrol, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
